opp.name: ZS_S2P_2026
opp.next_step: 6/25mc - Completed initial ZS alignment call with Artana Skereli regarding their upcoming Source-to-Pay evaluation tied to the broader SAP ECC to S/4HANA transformation. ZS is actively preparing an RFP and confirmed Zycus is one of the shortlisted vendors. Ariba remains in the running, but ZS intentionally wants to evaluate alternatives and is open to vendor input before the RFP is finalized. Immediate next step is a 30-minute meeting with Joe Carolina, who is spearheading the S/4HANA program and supervising the related procurement/S2P initiative. Artana wants Zycus to provide a concise company overview, relevant professional services/pharma customer examples, high-level S2P platform overview, AI capabilities/roadmap, SAP S/4HANA integration approach, and implementation model. RFP is expected to be finalized after the Joe meeting and new procurement executive review. Responses likely to have a 2 to 3 week window, followed by 4 to 5 hour demos, ideally onsite in Chicago. Project is approved, but no defined budget yet. Pricing will be evaluated based on ROI and market benchmarking. Phase 1 is expected to complement existing CLM, third-party risk, ESG, and SAP systems, with potential to expand the S2P footprint over time. Key risks include Ariba’s natural advantage due to SAP, other vendors influencing the RFP, new procurement executive starting July 6, lack of confirmed budget, and no direct CFO access yet. Next actions: schedule and prepare for Joe meeting, send Artana RFP consideration points, align internal Zycus team around SAP/S4 integration and AI roadmap, identify strongest professional services/pharma references, and push for a pre-RFP discovery session with finance, procurement, and IT.
opp.ais_why:
• ZS is 'evaluating S2P platforms alongside SAP migration' with the RFP 'focusing on integration, AI capabilities, and ROI' ‚Äî AI is explicitly one of three evaluation pillars.
• The full agentic suite (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) is scoped on a $400K deal, signaling AI capability is genuinely in the assessment.
• Agentic-platform-discussed is not yet set and the next step is scheduling a stakeholder meeting, so AI is a stated criterion but enthusiasm is not yet demonstrated.

metrics.narrative: ZS articulated the business problem as an SAP ECC to S/4HANA transformation requiring a complementary Source-to-Pay platform to work alongside existing CLM, third-party risk, ESG, and SAP systems. The need is tied to a broader ERP modernization program led by the relevant stakeholder, no quantified impact has been stated: no savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or error-rate reduction. The Next_Step__c field notes 'project is approved, but no defined budget yet' and 'pricing will be evaluated based on ROI and market benchmarking,' signaling the business case exists but financial metrics are not yet locked. The qualitative pain is SAP ECC end-of-life and the desire to avoid Ariba lock-in, but without a quantified ROI the deal lacks a measurable value anchor. Immediate move: secure the Joe Carolina meeting and use it to extract spend under management, transaction volume, and current process cost baseline.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (6/25 initial call summary, names Artana Skereli as the initial contact)
champion.narrative: Artana Skereli is the only named contact and is acting as a coordinator: she 'completed initial ZS alignment call' and is arranging the Joe Carolina meeting, indicating access and some advocacy (she confirmed Zycus is shortlisted and is open to vendor input before RFP finalization). However, there is no evidence of deep advocacy: no introductions beyond Joe, no shared internal docs, no pushing back on competitors, no confirmation she is influencing the RFP in Zycus's favor. Her role is likely procurement program manager or RFP coordinator, not Economic Buyer or Decision Maker. The Next_Step__c does not describe her as a champion; she is a point of contact. Joe Carolina is the program lead and may become a champion if the meeting goes well, but he has not yet engaged. The new procurement executive starting July 6 is unknown. Champion strength is developing at best: Artana is engaged but her influence and advocacy are unproven. Risk: no champion has been secured before RFP finalization, and the new procurement executive is a wild card. Immediate move: use the Joe meeting to assess his openness and influence, and secure Artana's or Joe's active advocacy by co-creating RFP criteria that favor Zycus (AI/Merlin, SAP integration, non-Ariba alternative).

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the confirmed competitor. The Next_Step__c states 'Ariba remains in the running, but ZS intentionally wants to evaluate alternatives and is open to vendor input before the RFP is finalized.' Ariba has a natural advantage because ZS is migrating to SAP S/4HANA, and Ariba is the SAP-native S2P solution. The Next_Step__c flags 'Ariba's natural advantage due to SAP' as a key risk, and also notes 'other vendors influencing the RFP' as a concern, implying additional competitors may be shortlisted but are not named. ZS's explicit desire to 'evaluate alternatives' and their openness to vendor input signals they are not sold on Ariba and may be looking for a reason to choose a non-SAP vendor. The wedge is Zycus's AI/Merlin capabilities, SAP S/4HANA integration as a best-of-breed alternative, and the ability to complement (not replace) ZS's existing CLM/risk/ESG systems without forcing an all-SAP stack. How we win: position Merlin and Agentic AI as differentiation Ariba cannot match, demonstrate deep S/4HANA integration that rivals native Ariba, deliver strong pharma/professional-services references, and co-create RFP criteria that reward AI innovation and flexibility over SAP-native convenience. Make the Joe meeting and pre-RFP discovery session the venues to embed these criteria before Ariba can influence the RFP.

identify_pain.narrative: ZS's pain is the SAP ECC to S/4HANA transformation, which is forcing a re-evaluation of their Source-to-Pay stack. The Next_Step__c states they are 'preparing an RFP' and want to 'evaluate alternatives' to Ariba, signaling dissatisfaction with or concern about Ariba lock-in as part of the SAP ecosystem. The pain owner is Joe Carolina, who is 'spearheading the S/4HANA program and supervising the related procurement/S2P initiative.' ZS's current stack includes CLM, third-party risk, ESG, and SAP systems, and they want Phase 1 of the S2P platform to 'complement' these, with 'potential to expand the S2P footprint over time,' indicating fragmented procurement processes and a desire for consolidation. The pain is operational (ERP migration, system fragmentation) and strategic (avoiding single-vendor lock-in). However, the pain is not yet quantified (no cost of delays, no current process inefficiency metrics). The Next_Step__c does not quote the buyer directly on pain; this is paraphrased by the rep. Immediate move: in the Joe meeting, get Joe or Artana to articulate the pain in their own words and quantify the cost of staying on ECC or continuing with fragmented S2P tools.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (6/25 initial call summary, notes 'implementation model' as a topic)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec) are completely unknown. No discussion of contract terms, redlining process, or legal approval has been recorded. ZS operates in professional services/pharma, which typically requires InfoSec and data-privacy reviews, but none are mentioned. No SI or implementation partner role has been defined; the Next_Step__c notes ZS wants to understand Zycus's 'implementation model,' implying this is still to be scoped. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function. The only paper-process signal is the structured RFP itself, which will likely include SOW and commercials, but the legal/procurement back-end is a black box. Immediate move: in the Joe meeting or a follow-up with procurement, map the contract approval process (legal, InfoSec, procurement operations), typical cycle time, and any required SI involvement.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. The Next_Step__c explicitly flags 'no direct CFO access yet' as a key risk. Artana Skereli appears to be a procurement or program contact coordinating the RFP, and Joe Carolina is named as the S/4HANA program lead supervising the S2P initiative, suggesting he may control or influence budget, but neither has been confirmed as the commercial decision-maker. A 'new procurement executive starting July 6' is mentioned, and this person may be the Economic Buyer or a gatekeeper to them. No Contact Roles have been created, and no Avoma calls show pricing or commercial conversations. The evidence shows budget authority is distributed or unconfirmed: the project is approved but budget is not defined, and pricing will be benchmarked. Immediate move: use the Joe Carolina meeting to map the approval chain and confirm who controls the S2P budget (CFO, CIO, the new procurement executive, or Joe himself).

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially mapped. ZS is running a formal RFP with a clear sequence: (1) vendor shortlisting (Zycus confirmed as shortlisted), (2) RFP finalization after the Joe Carolina meeting and new procurement executive review, (3) vendor responses (2-3 week window), (4) 4-5 hour demos (ideally onsite in Chicago), (5) vendor selection. The Next_Step__c notes 'new procurement executive starting July 6,' indicating a leadership change mid-process that may reset priorities or criteria. Joe Carolina is supervising the S2P initiative under the broader S/4HANA program, suggesting IT/ERP governance. The approval chain is not yet clear: the Next_Step__c flags 'lack of confirmed budget' and 'no direct CFO access yet,' meaning financial approval is still to be mapped. No evidence of who makes the final call (Joe, the new procurement exec, CFO, a committee). The process the buyer is running is vendor-evaluation-first, budget-lock-second, which is a risk. Immediate move: confirm the approval chain and decision timeline in the Joe meeting, and secure a path to the CFO and new procurement executive before RFP responses are due.

decision_criteria.narrative: ZS has shared high-level evaluation criteria implied by the RFP preparation agenda: company overview, relevant professional services/pharma customer examples, S2P platform overview, AI capabilities/roadmap, SAP S/4HANA integration approach, and implementation model. These are the topics Artana requested for the Joe meeting, signaling what ZS will evaluate in vendor responses. The Next_Step__c also states ZS is 'open to vendor input before the RFP is finalized,' meaning criteria are not yet locked. The buyer confirmed a structured process: RFP responses with a 2-3 week window, followed by 4-5 hour demos (ideally onsite in Chicago). However, the detailed scoring rubric, weighting, and must-have vs nice-to-have requirements are unknown. The decision criteria are forming but not documented. Immediate move: deliver the requested topics in the Joe meeting and push for a pre-RFP discovery session with finance, procurement, and IT to co-create the evaluation framework and embed Zycus strengths (AI, Merlin, SAP integration) as formal criteria.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — No AIS field data (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are all null). ZS explicitly requested 'AI capabilities/roadmap' as a Joe Carolina meeting topic, indicating at minimum AI Curious, possibly AI Hungry. The broader S/4HANA transformation context and the desire to modernize S2P suggest openness to innovation. However, no buyer conversation has validated AI appetite depth. Provisional read: AI Curious, with potential to be AI Hungry if the Merlin/Agentic AI story resonates.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 13 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 44 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 4 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 4 is anchored on the stage win (13), nudged by commitment 44 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

Zycus's product scope matches this (Products__c includes all named modules plus Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, iCompliance, ANA). The fit is strong on paper, but unproven in conversation: no demo, no technical validation, no customer references shared yet.

Ariba remains in the running, but ZS intentionally wants to evaluate alternatives and is open to vendor input before the RFP is finalized.

Immediate next step is a 30-minute meeting with Joe Carolina, who is spearheading the S/4HANA program and supervising the related procurement/S2P initiative. Artana wants Zycus to provide a concise company overview, relevant professional services/pharma customer examples, high-level S2P platform overview, AI capabilities/roadmap, SAP S/4HANA integration approach, and implementation model. RFP is expected to be finalized after the Joe meeting and new procurement executive review. Responses likely to have a 2 to 3 week window, followed by 4 to 5 hour demos, ideally onsite in Chicago.

Project is approved, but no defined budget yet. Pricing will be evaluated based on ROI and market benchmarking. Phase 1 is expected to complement existing CLM, third-party risk, ESG, and SAP systems, with potential to expand the S2P footprint over time. Key risks include Ariba’s natural advantage due to SAP, other vendors influencing the RFP, new procurement executive starting July 6, lack of confirmed budget, and no direct CFO access yet.


Next actions: schedule and prepare for Joe meeting, send Artana RFP consideration points, align internal Zycus team around SAP/S4 integration and AI roadmap, identify strongest professional services/pharma references, and push for a pre-RFP discovery session with finance, procurement, and IT.
